title: dryopteris arbuscula
intro: Dryopteris arbuscula, also known as dwarf wood fern, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Dryopteris arbuscula is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
